meaning: n.(用石架成的)史前墓石牌坊
examples: She is also standing next to a megalithic structure - a dolmen.
Shown here is a dolmen, a huge stone slab set upon other huge stones.
There was four guardian stones, looks like a dolmen on the step, but survived just one which you can see below.
The increasing popularity of Paganism is visible in the Dolmen Grove, a group of Dorset druids.
"This is a bit of a relief bounce after a very weak yesterday, " said Brian Gallagher, strategist at Dolmen Stockbrokers.
